info.title: Integration Toolkit API
info.description: API for integrating with external systems in a standardised way.

tags.name: trigger
tags.description: Endpoints to trigger ERP related actions

tags.name: integrations
tags.description: Integration and Use Case management endpoints

tags.name: managed-call
tags.description: Managed call endpoints for synchronous external API calls

post.description: Acknowledges an ERP tracking record by removing it from the tracking table, requires public authentication

ack_id.description: Unique identifier of the ERP tracking record to acknowledge

post.description: Triggers the ERP integration process

400.description: Bad request - invalid or missing parameters

post.description: Handles updates from ERP systems and tracks them appropriately

app_id.description: UUID that identifies the specific integration app instance

component_id.description: UUID that identifies the specific integration app component instance

meta.description: Metadata to be passed along with the events

correlation_id.description: ID that identifies the specific request for debugging purposes

events.description: List of ERP events to process

post.description:
Handles updates from ERP systems using integration_id directly.
This is the v2 version that simplifies the API by accepting integration_id
instead of app_id and component_id.

Handles updates from ERP systems using integration_id directly.
This is the v3 version that removes the unused event_type field and renames object_type to event_name
to align with the integration UI naming.

Test v2.0 mapping configuration by transforming a payload using the provided mapping rules without persisting data.

This endpoint accepts the same configuration format that is stored in the integration use case resource,
making it easier to test configurations before saving them to a use case.

See documentation at /docs/MAPPING_V2.md for detailed v2.0 format specification.

conditional_fields.description: Example showing how to conditionally enable/disable individual field mappings

422.description: Unprocessable entity - mapping configuration or payload validation failed

post.description:
Test mapping configuration by transforming a payload using the provided mapping rules without persisting data.

Supports both v1.0 (object-based) and v2.0 (event-based) mapping formats.
See documentation at /docs/MAPPING_V2.md for detailed v2.0 format specification.

v2_append_operation.description: Example showing how to use _append to add relations to existing entities

v2_field_enabled.description: Example showing how to conditionally enable/disable individual field mappings using the enabled property

422.description: Unprocessable entity - mapping configuration or payload validation failed

get.description: Retrieve all integrations for the authenticated organization

post.description: Create a new integration configuration

get.description: Retrieve a specific integration by its ID

delete.description: Delete an integration and all its use cases

post.description: Query events for a specific integration
Replay one or more events for a specific integration. Events will be re-processed with their original payloads but with a new correlation ID for traceability.

get.description: Retrieve all use cases for a specific integration

post.description: Create a new use case for an integration

get.description: Retrieve a specific use case by its ID

put.description: Update an existing use case configuration

delete.description: Delete a use case from an integration

get.description:
Retrieve historical versions of a use case's configuration.
History entries are returned in reverse chronological order (newest first).
Use the 'cursor' parameter for pagination to fetch additional entries.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Opaque pagination cursor. Pass the 'next_cursor' value from a previous
response to fetch the next page of results.

history.description: History entries in reverse chronological order (newest first)

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for fetching the next page. Absent if no more pages.

get.description: Retrieve all integrations with embedded use cases for the authenticated organization

200.description: Successfully retrieved integrations with use cases

post.description: Create a new integration with embedded use cases.

201.description: Integration created successfully with use cases

get.description: Retrieve a specific integration with all its embedded use cases

200.description: Successfully retrieved integration with use cases

put.description:
Update an existing integration with embedded use cases.
The integration must already exist.
Use cases are updated declaratively:
- Use cases in the request with matching IDs are updated
- Use cases in the request without matching IDs are created
- Existing use cases not in the request are deleted

200.description: Integration updated successfully with use cases

delete.description: Delete an integration and all its use cases

get.description:
Returns the current allowed_domains, allowed_ips, and vpc_mode for a secure_proxy use case.
Staff-only — gated by internal-auth issuer AND admin-portal Cognito user pool membership.
Rejects Login-As tokens.

404.description: Use case not found or not of type secure_proxy

put.description:
Replaces allowed_domains and/or allowed_ips on a secure_proxy use case.
At least one of the two fields is required. Validation mirrors the CLI's
`validateDomainPatterns` / `validateCidrs`. Writes a USECASE_HISTORY row
with the admin user's email as `changed_by`.

Update semantics per field:
  - **omitted** — field is not modified; the stored value is preserved.
  - **non-empty array** — the stored value is replaced with the supplied list.
  - **empty array (`[]`)** — the list is cleared (stored as `[]`). This is
    the canonical way to remove all entries. `null` is not accepted.

Staff-only — same auth gates as GET.

404.description: Use case not found or not of type secure_proxy

get.summary: List secure_proxy whitelist change history (admin portal only)
get.description:
Returns the most recent USECASE_HISTORY entries for a secure_proxy use case,
in reverse chronological order (newest first). Each entry includes the
actor email (`changed_by`), the ISO-8601 timestamp (`history_created_at`),
the `change_description` (free-text action), and the full `configuration`
snapshot — from which UI-08 computes a before/after diff between consecutive
entries.

Staff-only — gated by internal-auth issuer AND admin-portal Cognito user pool
membership. Rejects Login-As tokens (same auth gate as the GET / PUT
secure-proxy-whitelist operations).

Thin wrapper over the service-layer `listUseCaseHistory` that powers
`GET /v1/integrations/{integrationId}/use-cases/{useCaseId}/history` — the
separate path exists because the /v1 variant is tenant-gated and admin-portal
internal-auth tokens do not carry tenant permissions.

parameters.name: useCaseId
parameters.description: The use case ID (must be of type secure_proxy)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Maximum number of history entries to return. Default 10, max 50.
Capped at the service-layer page size (20) so `limit > 20` is silently
clamped to 20. UI-08 requests 5-10 for the panel view.

history.description:
Entries are `SecureProxyUseCaseHistoryEntry` in reverse
chronological order (newest first). Entries older than the
requested `limit` are not returned.

404.description: Use case not found or not of type secure_proxy

put.description:
Creates or updates a mapping from an app/component to an integration.
This allows ERP updates sent via app_id and component_id to be associated
with a specific integration configuration.

parameters.name: integrationId
parameters.description: The integration ID to map to

409.description: Mapping already exists (use overwrite=true to replace)

delete.description: Removes a mapping from an app/component to an integration.

parameters.name: integrationId
parameters.description: The integration ID (used for authorization, must match the mapping)

post.description:
Query inbound monitoring events for a specific integration.
Returns detailed information about inbound sync events from ERP systems,
including success rates, error breakdowns, and processing metrics.

Get aggregated statistics for both inbound and outbound monitoring events for a specific integration.
Returns summary metrics for inbound (ERP sync) and outbound (webhook delivery) events,
including success/error counts and optional breakdowns.

Get time-series aggregated event counts for monitoring charts.
Returns pre-bucketed counts at configurable intervals for both inbound and outbound events.
Maximum of 200 buckets per request. Returns 400 if the time range and interval would exceed this limit.

get.description:
Get the status of all outbound use cases for a specific integration.
Returns conflict information when events or webhooks are disabled but the use case is enabled.

post.description:
Poll outbound messages for an integration's poll-mode use cases.
Takes a lease on the head-of-line batch of the integration's FIFO stream:
the returned messages stay invisible to subsequent polls until they are
acknowledged or the visibility timeout elapses. POST because taking a
lease mutates server state — auto-retrying middleware must not burn
leases. One in-flight batch per stream: while a lease is active (or
another poll wins the race), the response is an empty batch
(`messages: []`), not an error. Requires the `integration:consume` grant.

200.description: Leased batch of outbound messages (possibly empty)

post.description:
Acknowledge polled outbound messages. Acks are validated against the
active lease and committed as a prefix-contiguous cursor advance:
messages must be acknowledged in stream order. Out-of-order acks past
the first gap are rejected per id (`out_of_order`), acks with an
outdated lease token are rejected (`stale_lease`), and unknown ids are
rejected (`not_found`). Acknowledged messages are never delivered
again. Requires the `integration:consume` grant.

get.description:
List an integration's dead-lettered outbound queue messages
(poison_policy enforcement and operator skips move messages here).
Operator endpoint — requires the `integration:manage` grant. Message
payloads are NOT included in listings: entries are keyed by the
opaque message id and carry delivery metadata only. Paginated via an
opaque `next_token`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of DLQ entries to return

parameters.name: next_token
parameters.description: Opaque pagination token from a previous response

post.description:
Redrive selected dead-lettered messages back into the live stream.
Operator endpoint — requires the `integration:manage` grant.
A redriven message is re-enqueued at the tail with a new id and
sequence — it is delivered out of its original per-entity order (the
stream has moved on); this is inherent to redrive and matches SQS DLQ
semantics. The redriven copy starts with zero delivery attempts and a
fresh retention window; the original DLQ entry is removed. Per-id
results report `redriven` or `not_found` (unknown ids, or entries
concurrently redriven/expired).

Unblock an integration's outbound stream halted by the `block`
poison policy: skips (dead-letters) the current blocked head message,
emitting MSG_DEAD_LETTERED and letting the next message become the
head. Operator endpoint — requires the `integration:manage` grant.
Returns `unblocked: false` as a no-op when the stream is not
currently blocked (or the state moved concurrently) — safe to retry.

Query API access logs for a specific integration's organization.
Returns access token usage analytics filtered by user_id (access token).
Supports infinite scroll pagination with cursor-based navigation.

parameters.name: integrationId
parameters.description: The integration ID (used for tenant authorization)

post.description:
Query outbound monitoring events for a specific integration.
Returns detailed information about outbound event deliveries,
filtered by event_name (event_catalog_event) linked to the integration's outbound use cases.

Query monitoring events from the unified erp_monitoring_v2 table.
Returns all event types (inbound, outbound, file_proxy, etc.) in a single list.
Replaces the separate v1 inbound-events and outbound-events endpoints.

Get aggregated statistics from the unified erp_monitoring_v2 table.
Returns combined metrics for all event types with optional breakdowns.

Get time-series aggregated event counts from the unified erp_monitoring_v2 table.
Returns bucketed counts for chart rendering.

get.description:
Returns all monitoring events sharing the same event_id, ordered chronologically.
Also includes the original inbound event payload from erp_incoming_events if available.
Used to display a full event trace/timeline.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: The event ID to get associated events for

get.summary: List all secure proxy use cases
get.description:
Lists all secure_proxy use cases across all integrations for the authenticated organization.
Returns minimal data suitable for dropdowns and selection UIs.

200.description: Successfully retrieved secure proxy use cases

post.summary: Proxy HTTP request through secure VPC
post.description:
Routes an HTTP request through a VPC with either static IP egress or VPN secure link access.
The VPC mode is determined by the referenced secure_proxy use case configuration.
For secure_link mode, the target URL must match the use case's allowed_domains whitelist.

200.description: Proxied response from the target URL

403.description: Forbidden - insufficient permissions or target domain not whitelisted

502.description: Upstream error from the proxied target

503.description: VPC proxy not available for the requested mode

post.description:
Execute a managed call operation synchronously. The slug in the path acts as the RPC method name.
Calls an external partner API with JSONata mapping on both request and response.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Use case slug (acts as the RPC method name)

200.description:
Managed call executed successfully.
The response body is the JSONata-mapped result (or raw external API response if no mapping).
If inbound routing is configured, check X-Inbound-Event-Id header for tracking.

X-Inbound-Event-Id.description: Event ID for tracking inbound pipeline processing (only present when inbound routing is configured)

400.description: Bad request (e.g., invalid URL blocked by SSRF protection)

502.description: External API error (upstream service returned an error)

post.summary: Preview scaffolded types for a connector integration
post.description: Analyses the JSONata mappings of all managed-call use cases in the integration and returns scaffolded type descriptors. The frontend uses these to show the type editor modal where developers fill in leaf types.

post.summary: Generate a TypeScript npm package for a connector integration
post.description: Generates a complete TypeScript npm package with typed interfaces for all managed-call use cases. This is a stateless operation that does not persist any changes. Use the commit-types endpoint to lock configurations after review.

post.summary: Commit generated types and lock use case configurations
post.description: Commits the generated types by locking use case configurations and updating version tracking. Should be called after the user reviews and downloads the generated package.

EpilotAuth.description: Authorization header with epilot OAuth2 bearer token

object_type.description: Type of the object being updated (business_partner, contract_account, etc.). Corresponds to "Event Name" from the integration UI.

oneOf.description: The serialized object data payload (JSON, XML, etc.) as a string
Direct JSON object (will be automatically serialized)

payload.description: The object data payload - can be either a serialized string or a direct JSON object

deduplication_id.description: Optional unique identifier for idempotency - prevents duplicate processing of the same event within 24 hours in context of the same integration. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores.

use_case_id.description: Resolved use case ID for the inbound event. Null when no use case matched or for events ingested before this field was introduced. Server-populated only — ignored if supplied on inbound requests.

integration_id.description: UUID that identifies the integration configuration to use

correlation_id.description: Optional ID that identifies the specific request for debugging purposes

events.description: List of ERP events to process

event_name.description: Event name from integration mapping (e.g., business_partner, contract_account). Required when use_case_slug is not provided.

oneOf.description: The serialized object data payload (JSON, XML, etc.) as a string
Direct JSON object (will be automatically serialized)

payload.description: The object data payload - can be either a serialized string or a direct JSON object

use_case_slug.description: Recommended. Use case slug for routing this event to the correct use case configuration. If provided, takes precedence over event_name for use case lookup. Preferred over event_name-based routing as slugs are portable across environments.

deduplication_id.description: Optional unique identifier for idempotency - prevents duplicate processing of the same event within 24 hours in context of the same integration. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores.

integration_id.description: UUID that identifies the integration configuration to use

correlation_id.description: Optional ID that identifies the specific request for debugging purposes

group_id.description:
Controls ordering and parallelism for this request's events.

By default, all events for a given `integration_id` are processed
**strictly in order, one at a time**. For high-volume integrations this
can become a throughput bottleneck.

Set `group_id` to opt into **parallel processing**:
- Events sharing the same `group_id` are processed in the order received.
- Events with different `group_id` values are processed in parallel.

Typical usage is to derive `group_id` from a logical partition key in
your payload — for example the customer ID, contract ID, or meter ID —
so updates to the same business object remain ordered while unrelated
objects are processed concurrently.

Notes:
- Up to 20 groups per integration are processed concurrently. Using
  more distinct values than that yields no additional parallelism.
- Omit this field if strict per-integration ordering is required.

events.description: List of ERP events to process

execution_id.description: Unique identifier of the current automation execution

org_id.description: Identifier of the organization where the automation is executed

webhook_id.description: Identifier of the self-service webhook configuration

flow_id.description: Identifier of the automation flow that triggered the action

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the webhook event was created

action_id.description: Identifier of the automation action being executed

flow_action_id.description: Identifier of the specific automation flow action instance

flow_name.description: Human readable name of the automation flow

activity_id.description: Identifier of the entity activity related to this trigger

entity_id.description: Identifier of the entity referenced by the activity

access_token_ids.description: List of access token IDs associated with this integration

app_ids.description: List of app IDs associated with this integration

environment_config.description: Configuration defining environment variables needed by this integration. Values are stored in the Environments API.

integration_type.description: Type of integration. "erp" is the ERP integration with inbound/outbound use cases. "connector" is for complex proxy integrations with external APIs.

protected.description: If true, integration is displayed in read-only mode in the UI to discourage changes

_manifest.description: The manifest IDs associated with this integration

base_url.description: Base URL for the partner API

types_versions.description: History of generated type package versions

ManagedCallAuth.description: Authentication configuration for managed call requests

token_url.description: OAuth2 token URL. Can be plain text or {{env.key}} reference.

client_id.description: OAuth2 client ID. Can be plain text or {{env.key}} reference.

client_secret.description: OAuth2 client secret. Must be an {{env.key}} reference (secret).

audience.description: OAuth2 audience parameter (e.g. for Auth0, Azure AD). Can be plain text or {{env.key}} reference.

resource.description: OAuth2 resource parameter (e.g. for Azure AD). Can be plain text or {{env.key}} reference.

body_params.description: Additional key-value pairs for the OAuth2 token request body. Values can be {{env.key}} references.

headers.description: Additional headers for the OAuth2 token request. Values can be {{env.key}} references.

query_params.description: Additional query parameters for the OAuth2 token URL. Values can be {{env.key}} references.

api_key_header.description: Header name for API key auth (default X-API-Key)

api_key.description: API key value. Must be an {{env.key}} reference (secret).

token.description: Bearer token value. Must be an {{env.key}} reference (secret).

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the integration was created

updated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the integration was last updated

key.description: Environment variable key, used to look up the value in the Environments API.

label.description: Display label for the field in the UI

type.description: Whether the value is a plain string or an encrypted secret

description.description: Help text shown below the field

required.description: Whether this field must be filled before the integration can be used

order.description: Sort order for display and drag-to-reorder

AutoRefreshSettings.description: Auto-refresh settings for keeping integration data fresh

freshnessThresholdMinutes.description: Maximum age (in minutes) of data before it is considered stale and eligible for refresh

app_id.description: UUID of the integration app instance

component_id.description: UUID of the integration app component instance

overwrite.description: If true, overwrites existing mapping. If false and mapping exists, returns 409 Conflict.

app_id.description: UUID of the integration app instance

component_id.description: UUID of the integration app component instance

integration_id.description: The integration ID this app/component is mapped to

allOf.description: Integration with embedded use cases for atomic CRUD operations

use_cases.description: All use cases belonging to this integration

allOf.description:
Request to create or update an integration with embedded use cases (upsert).
This is a declarative operation - the request represents the desired state.

use_cases.description:
Full list of use cases (declarative). This replaces ALL existing use cases.
- Use cases with an `id` field matching an existing use case will be updated
- Use cases without an `id` or with a non-matching `id` will be created
- Existing use cases not in this list will be deleted

InboundIntegrationEventConfiguration.description: Configuration for inbound use cases (ERP to epilot)

entities.description: Array of entity configurations for this event

meter_readings.description: Array of meter reading configurations for this event

OutboundIntegrationEventConfiguration.description: Configuration for outbound use cases. Defines the event that triggers the flow and the webhook mappings.

event_catalog_event.description: The Event Catalog event name that triggers this outbound flow

mappings.description: List of mappings that transform and deliver the event

unique_ids.description:
Array of attribute names that uniquely identify this entity.
The _type hint for repeatable fields (e.g., email, phone) should be specified
on the corresponding field definition in the fields array.

jsonataExpression.description: Optional JSONata expression to pre-process the event data before field mapping

enabled.description: Controls whether this entity mapping should be processed. Can be a boolean or a JSONata expression (string) that evaluates to a boolean.

mode.description:
Operation mode for entity mapping:
- 'upsert': Create or update the entity (default)
- 'delete': Soft delete the entity (marks as deleted)
- 'purge': Hard delete the entity (permanent removal)
- 'upsert-prune-scope-purge': Upsert entities from array, then purge entities in scope that weren't upserted
- 'upsert-prune-scope-delete': Upsert entities from array, then soft delete entities in scope that weren't upserted

jsonataExpression.description:
Optional JSONata expression to extract meter reading items from the event data.
If not provided, the entire payload is used as the reading data.
Useful when you need to extract an array of readings from a nested structure (e.g., "$.readings").

reading_matching.description:
Strategy for matching incoming readings against existing readings.
- 'external_id': Match readings by external_id attribute (default behavior)
- 'strict-date': Match by meter_id + counter_id + direction + date (German timezone).
  Useful when readings originate from ECP and are echoed back by the ERP with truncated timestamps.

mode.description:
Operation mode for meter reading mapping:
- 'upsert': Create or update meter readings (default)
- 'delete': Delete the meter reading
- 'upsert-prune-scope': Upsert readings from array, then delete all other readings for the same meter+counter that weren't upserted

fields.description: Field mapping definitions for meter reading attributes

PruneScopeConfig.description:
Scope configuration for upsert-prune-scope modes.
Defines how to find entities that should be pruned if not in the upsert payload.
The scope is resolved against the original event payload (not individual array items).

scope_mode.description:
Scope mode for finding entities to prune:
- 'relations': Find scope by looking at all entities related to a specific entity (both direct and reverse relations)
- 'query': Find scope entities directly via query parameters

schema.description:
For 'relations' mode: The schema of the entity to find (e.g., 'billing_account').
Not used for 'query' mode.

unique_ids.description:
For 'relations' mode: How to identify the scope entity from the payload.
Not used for 'query' mode.

query.description:
For 'query' mode: Direct query parameters to find scope entities.
Not used for 'relations' or 'reverse-relations' modes.

MeterReadingPruneScopeConfig.description:
Scope configuration for meter reading upsert-prune-scope mode.
The scope is all readings for the same meter + counter.

source.description:
Optional source filter. When set, only readings with this source
are eligible for pruning (e.g., 'ERP' to only prune ERP-synced readings).

unique_ids.description: Array of unique identifier field mappings

field.description: Source field name or JSONPath expression (if starts with $)

enabled.description: Controls whether this field mapping should be processed. Can be a boolean or a JSONata expression (string) that evaluates to a boolean. Defaults to true if omitted.

FileProxyUrlParam.description: Parameter for file proxy URL. Exactly one of field, constant, or jsonataExpression must be set.

field.description: Source field name or JSONPath expression (if starts with $)

constant.description: Constant value (any type, stringified for URL)

FileProxyUrlParams.description: Custom query parameters. Keys become URL param names, values resolved from payload.

FileProxyUrlConfig.description: Auto-constructs a file proxy download URL. orgId and integrationId are injected from context. Exactly one of use_case_id or use_case_slug must be provided. Using use_case_slug is recommended as it is portable across environments.

use_case_slug.description: Recommended. Slug of the file_proxy use case. Maps to useCaseSlug query parameter. Portable across environments.

use_case_id.description: Legacy. UUID of the file_proxy use case. Maps to useCaseId query parameter. Prefer use_case_slug for portable configuration.

PortalOrigin.description: Origin/type of an epilot portal configuration.

PortalRefFilter.description: Filter applied to the org's portal configurations before selection. All filters default to "match any" except `enabled` (default `true`) and `is_dummy` (default `false`). Set `enabled` or `is_dummy` to `null` to opt out of the default.

origin.description: Single origin or array of origins (matches if origin is in the array).

enabled.description: Match portals with this `enabled` value. Default `true`. Set to `null` to ignore.

is_dummy.description: Match portals with this `is_dummy` value. Default `false`. Set to `null` to ignore.

PortalRefConfig.description: Resolves to a property of one of the calling organization's epilot portal configurations at runtime, replacing hard-coded environment-specific portal UUIDs in inbound mappings. Matched portals are sorted ascending by `(_created_at, portal_id)`; portals without `_created_at` sort first (treated as oldest). When `select: "single"` matches more than one portal, the resolver still returns the oldest match and emits a `PORTAL_REF_AMBIGUOUS` warning.

select.description: `single` returns one literal value (the oldest matching portal); `all` returns an array of literal values (0, 1, or many).

return.description: Evaluate the sibling `jsonataExpression` against the matched portal(s). With `select: "single"` the input is the matched PortalConfig object; with `select: "all"` it is the full filtered+sorted array.

jsonataExpression.description: JSONata expression evaluated against the matched portal(s).

EnvVarRefConfig.description: Resolves to an org-scoped environment variable from the epilot environments-api service at runtime, replacing hard-coded environment-specific values (URLs, prefixes, identifiers) in inbound mappings. Secrets (`SecretString` values) are never exposed; the runtime treats both "missing key" and "secret-typed key" as identical `undefined` outcomes (no info disclosure via error code). For secret-resolving contexts (e.g. authorization headers in managed-call or file-proxy step configurations), use the templated `{{ env.<key> }}` syntax instead — that mechanism does decrypt secrets.

key.description: Environment variable key. Must match the environments-api key contract (lowercase, digits, `_`, `.`, `-`; max 128 chars; starts with a lowercase letter or digit). Supports dot-namespaced keys like `erp_api.base_url`.

default.description: Literal string returned when the key is missing (or is a secret). When provided, the `ENV_VAR_REF_NOT_FOUND` warning is suppressed because the author signalled the absence is expected.

return.description: `value` returns the resolved env-var string as-is.
Evaluate the sibling `jsonataExpression` against the resolved env-var value (the input `$` is the string value).

jsonataExpression.description: JSONata expression evaluated against the resolved env-var value.

id.description:
Optional use case ID for update matching.
- If provided and matches an existing use case, that use case is updated
- If provided but no match, a new use case with this ID is created
- If omitted, a new use case with auto-generated ID is created

slug.description: URL-safe identifier for the use case. Optional on this upsert/sync endpoint — when omitted, the server derives one from the name and ensures uniqueness within the integration. Immutable after creation.

enabled.description: Whether the use case is enabled

change_description.description: Optional description of this change (like a commit message)

type.description: Use case type for managed API calls

id.description: Unique identifier for the use case

slug.description: URL-safe identifier for the use case. Recommended for portable cross-environment referencing. Unique per integration. Immutable after creation. Lowercase alphanumeric, hyphens, and underscores only.

change_description.description: Description of the last change made to this use case

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the use case was created

updated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the use case was last updated

type.description: Use case type for managed API calls

types_locked.description: Whether types have been generated for this use case

slug.description: URL-safe identifier for the use case. Required for explicit creates so every use case has a portable cross-environment identifier. Must be unique per integration. Immutable after creation. Lowercase alphanumeric, hyphens, and underscores only.

enabled.description: Whether the use case is enabled

type.description: Use case type for managed API calls

slug.description: URL-safe identifier for the use case. Recommended for portable cross-environment referencing. Can only be set once on use cases that don't have a slug yet. Immutable after being set.

enabled.description: Whether the use case is enabled

change_description.description: Optional description of this change (like a commit message)

type.description: Use case type for managed API calls

id.description: Unique identifier for this history entry

useCaseId.description: Reference to the parent use case

name.description: Use case name at this point in history

slug.description: Use case slug at this point in history

enabled.description: Whether the use case was enabled at this point in history

change_description.description: Description of the change that was made at this point in history

changed_by.description: User ID of the user who made the change that produced this history entry

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the use case was originally created

updated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of this historical snapshot (before the update)

history_created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this history entry was created

type.description: Use case type for managed API calls

types_locked.description: Whether types have been generated for this use case

SecureProxyUseCaseConfiguration.description: Configuration for secure_proxy use cases. Defines how to route requests through a secure VPC.

vpc_mode.description:
VPC routing mode. Read-only after creation.
- static_ip: Routes through a VPC with static outbound IP (NAT Gateway) for IP-allowlisted external APIs.
- secure_link: Routes through a VPN VPC for accessing private customer networks.

allowed_domains.description:
Domain whitelist for secure_link mode. Admin-only — can only be modified directly in DynamoDB via admin script.
Supports exact match (e.g., "api.wemag.com") and wildcard prefix (e.g., "*.wemag.com").

allowed_ips.description:
IP allowlist (CIDR notation) for secure_link mode. Admin-only — can only be modified directly in DynamoDB via admin script.
Required for secure_link mode. All DNS-resolved IPs must match at least one range.
Example: ["10.0.1.0/24", "192.168.1.0/24"]

SecureProxyWhitelist.description:
Current whitelist state for a secure_proxy use case. vpc_mode is read-only
context so the UI can show the user what mode the pool is in.

vpc_mode.description: VPC routing mode (read-only). `null` if not yet set.

allowed_domains.description:
Exact domain ("api.example.com") or wildcard prefix ("*.example.com").
Wildcards must have at least 2 labels in the suffix.

SecureProxyWhitelistUpdate.description:
Partial update for a secure_proxy whitelist. At least one of
`allowed_domains` or `allowed_ips` must be provided.

Per-field semantics:
  - **omitted** — the field is not modified; the stored value is preserved.
  - **non-empty array** — the stored value is replaced with the supplied list.
  - **empty array (`[]`)** — the list is cleared (stored as `[]`). This is the
    canonical way to remove all entries from a list. `null` is not accepted.

allowed_domains.description:
Exact domain ("api.example.com") or wildcard prefix ("*.example.com").
Wildcards must have at least 2 labels in the suffix.
Pass `[]` to clear all allowed domains.

allowed_ips.description:
CIDR-notation IP ranges, e.g. "10.0.0.0/24".
Pass `[]` to clear all allowed IPs.

integration_id.description: Integration ID that owns the secure_proxy use case

use_case_id.description: Use case ID (provide either use_case_id or use_case_slug)

use_case_slug.description: Use case slug (provide either use_case_id or use_case_slug)

url.description: Target URL to proxy the request to

status_code.description: HTTP status code from the upstream response

ManagedCallOperationConfig.description: Configuration for managed_call use cases. Defines a single API operation with JSONata mapping.

request_mapping.description: JSONata expression for outbound body transformation

response_mapping.description: JSONata expression for inbound response transformation

inbound_use_case_slug.description:
Slug of the inbound use case to route responses to for async entity processing.
When set, the managed call response is queued to the inbound pipeline and processed
using the referenced inbound use case's mapping configuration.

ManagedCallOperation.description: HTTP operation configuration for managed calls

headers.description: Custom HTTP headers for the request. Values support {{variable}} interpolation from the request payload and {{env.VAR}} references for environment variables.

query_params.description: Query parameters for the request. Values support {{variable}} interpolation from the request payload.

payload.description: Request payload for the managed call operation

correlation_id.description: Correlation ID for tracing related events (auto-generated if not provided)

ManagedCallExecuteResponse.description:
The response from a managed call execution.
On success, returns the JSONata-mapped response data directly (no wrapper).
The shape is entirely defined by your response_mapping JSONata expression.
If no response_mapping is configured, returns the raw external API response.
Check the X-Inbound-Event-Id header for inbound pipeline tracking when inbound routing is configured.

details.description: Additional error details (e.g., status code and body for external API errors)

TypeAnnotations.description: Developer-provided type annotations for a use case's request and response fields

request.description: Type annotations for request fields, keyed by dot-path (e.g., "vendors[].id" -> "string")

TypeDescriptor.description: Describes the inferred type shape of a JSONata expression

properties.description: For kind=object, the properties and their type descriptors

items.description: For kind=array, the type of array items

source.description: For kind=unknown, the JSONata path that produced this value

UseCaseTypePreview.description: Scaffolded type descriptors for a single use case

suggested_version.description: Suggested next version based on detected changes

domain_map_name.description: Name of the domain UseCaseMap to extend

annotations.description: Type annotations per use case slug

files.description: Generated file contents keyed by file path

annotations.description: Type annotations per use case slug

FileProxyUseCaseConfiguration.description:
Configuration for file_proxy use cases. Defines how to authenticate and fetch files from external document systems.

The file proxy download URL always requires `orgId`, `integrationId`, and either `useCaseSlug` (recommended) or `useCaseId` (legacy UUID) as query parameters.
The `orgId` is included in the signed URL to establish organization context without requiring authentication.
Additional use-case-specific parameters are declared in the `params` array.

secure_proxy.description:
Optional secure proxy attachment for routing all outbound file proxy requests.
Only `use_case_slug` is supported and the referenced secure_proxy use case
must belong to the same integration.

params.description: Additional use-case-specific parameters expected in the download URL query string (beyond the required orgId, integrationId, and useCaseSlug or useCaseId)

allowed_origins.description: Additional origins permitted to call /download for this use case (CORS, exact match). Portal origins are always allowed.

steps.description: Ordered list of HTTP steps to execute to retrieve the file

use_case_slug.description: Slug of the secure_proxy use case in the same integration.

token_url.description: Handlebars template for the OAuth2 token endpoint URL

client_id.description: Handlebars template for the OAuth2 client ID

client_secret.description: Handlebars template for the OAuth2 client secret

audience.description: Handlebars template for the OAuth2 audience parameter

resource.description: Handlebars template for the OAuth2 resource parameter

username.description: Handlebars template for the OAuth2 resource owner username. Required when type is oauth2_password.

password.description: Handlebars template for the OAuth2 resource owner password. Required when type is oauth2_password.

body_params.description: Additional key-value pairs to include in the token request body. Values support Handlebars templates.

headers.description: Additional headers to include in the token request. Values support Handlebars templates.

query_params.description: Additional query parameters to append to the token URL. Values support Handlebars templates.

name.description: Parameter name as it appears in the query string

url.description: Handlebars template for the request URL

body.description: Handlebars template for the request body (POST only)
JSONata expression to extract file content from step results

filename.description: JSONata expression to extract the filename

content_type.description: JSONata expression to extract the content type

object_type.description:
Type of the object/event being mapped.
For v1.0: must match a key in mapping_configuration.mapping.objects
For v2.0: must match a key in mapping_configuration.mapping.events

oneOf.description: The serialized object data payload (JSON, XML, etc.) as a string
Direct JSON object (will be automatically serialized)

payload.description: The object data payload - can be either a serialized string or a direct JSON object

MappingSimulationV2Request.description:
Request for v2 mapping simulation. Uses the same configuration format stored in integration use case resources,
making it easier to test configurations before saving them.

oneOf.description: The serialized event data payload (JSON, XML, etc.) as a string
Direct JSON object (will be automatically serialized)

payload.description: The event data payload - can be either a serialized string or a direct JSON object

warnings.description: Validation warnings about the configuration (e.g., unique_ids referencing non-indexed fields)

entity_schema.description: The entity schema slug where the issue was found

field.description: The field name that caused the warning

unique_identifiers.description: Unique identifier mappings for this entity

$entity_unique_ids.description: Unique identifiers for the meter counter

attributes.description: Meter reading attributes. Required: external_id, timestamp, source, value. `timestamp` must be ISO 8601 — either `YYYY-MM-DD` or `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` (with optional fractional seconds and optional `Z` / `±HH:mm` timezone offset); non-ISO formats (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY` or epoch numbers) are rejected and must be converted upstream via a `jsonataExpression` (e.g. `$fromMillis(...)`). Date-only values are normalized to midnight UTC and offset-less date-times are anchored to UTC before being forwarded to the metering API. `source` must be one of: ECP, ERP, 360, journey-submission. `reason` (optional) must be one of: regular, irregular, last, first, meter_change, contract_change, meter_adjustment (or empty/null).

unique_ids.description: Mapping of entity types to their unique identifier field mappings

items.description: List of local keys (must be present under the mapping "fields"!)

oneOf.description: Map of remote keys to local entity keys

field.description: Source field name (mutually exclusive with jsonataExpression)

jsonataExpression.description: JSONata expression for transformation (mutually exclusive with field)

OutboundMapping.description: A mapping that delivers an event to an external system — either pushed to a webhook (with a JSONata payload transformation) or made available on the pull-based poll queue (raw event payload, no transformation)

jsonata_expression.description: JSONata expression to transform the event payload. Required for webhook delivery; ignored for poll delivery.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the mapping was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the mapping was last updated

DeliveryConfig.description: Configuration for how the event should be delivered. webhook = push delivery via svc-webhooks (JSONata-transformed payload); poll = pull-based queue delivery where the consumer fetches items via the poll API (raw event payload)

WebhookDeliveryConfig.description: Push delivery of the transformed event to a webhook via svc-webhooks

webhook_id.description: Reference to the webhook configuration in svc-webhooks

webhook_name.description: Cached webhook name for display purposes

webhook_url.description: Cached webhook URL for display purposes

PollDeliveryConfig.description: Pull-based queue delivery. Items carry the raw standardized event-catalog payload; no JSONata mapping is applied in poll mode. Consumers fetch and acknowledge items via the poll API.

retention_days.description: How long undelivered queue items are retained before expiry

poison_policy.description: What happens when an item exhausts max_delivery_attempts: dead_letter routes the exhausted item to the dead-letter queue; block halts the queue until operator/consumer action. Enforcement lands with the queue consumer (Phase 10) — this field defines the contract.

max_delivery_attempts.description: Maximum delivery attempts before the poison_policy is applied

useCaseId.description: Unique identifier for the use case

name.description: Human-readable name of the use case

useCaseEnabled.description: Whether the use case is enabled

eventCatalogEvent.description: The Event Catalog event name that triggers this outbound flow

eventEnabled.description: Whether the event is enabled in Event Catalog. Null if the API is unreachable.

status.description:
Overall status of the use case:
- 'ok': Use case is enabled and all dependencies are properly configured
- 'conflict': Use case has configuration issues (disabled events/webhooks while enabled)
- 'disabled': Use case is disabled

conflicts.description: List of detected conflicts, if any

OutboundPollStatus.description:
Queue/consumer health for a poll-mode use case. Present only on use
cases with a poll delivery mapping — webhook-only use cases are
unaffected. Depth/age/DLQ numbers are first-page approximations.

queue_depth.description: Unconsumed messages attributable to this use case (first-page approximation)

oldest_unconsumed_age_seconds.description: Age of the oldest unconsumed message in seconds — null when the queue is empty

last_poll_at.description: Timestamp of the last successful poll lease — null before the first poll

last_ack_at.description: Timestamp of the last committed acknowledgment — null before the first ack

blocked.description:
Whether the integration's outbound stream is currently halted by a
blocked head message (poison_policy 'block'). Stream-level flag —
the same value is reported on every poll use case of the
integration, because the stream spans them.

dlq_count.description: Dead-lettered messages of this use case awaiting redrive or expiry (first-page approximation)

enabled.description: Whether the webhook is enabled. Null if the API is unreachable.

type.description:
Type of conflict:
- 'event_disabled': Event catalog event is disabled while use case is enabled
- 'all_webhooks_disabled': All webhooks are disabled while use case is enabled
- 'event_enabled_while_disabled': Event is enabled while use case is disabled
- 'webhook_enabled_while_disabled': A webhook is enabled while use case is disabled
- 'stream_blocked': The outbound stream is halted by a poison message awaiting operator action or consumer ack (poll mode)
- 'dlq_items_present': Dead-lettered messages await redrive or expiry (poll mode)

webhookId.description: Webhook ID (only present for webhook_enabled_while_disabled conflicts)

limit.description:
Maximum number of messages to lease in this batch. The ~5.5 MB
response cap may truncate the batch earlier when payloads are
large — the leased run shrinks accordingly.

id.description: Opaque message id (msg_…) — stable per message across leases

lease_token.description: Opaque lease token (lt_…) — echo back on ack

use_case_id.description: The poll-mode use case that produced this message

event_id.description: Unique id of the originating event

group.description:
Ordering group — messages sharing a group are strictly ordered,
distinct groups are independent. Constant "0" in v1.

payload.description: The raw standardized event-catalog event, always inlined as-is

messages.description: The leased batch in strict stream order (empty when contended or drained)

visibility_timeout_seconds.description:
Effective visibility timeout for this lease — a per-integration
server-side setting (default 300 seconds)

has_more.description: Whether more messages are available beyond this batch

lease_token.description: The lease token (lt_…) returned by the poll that leased this message

reason.description: Rejection reason — present only when status is rejected

id.description: Opaque message id (msg_…) of the dead-lettered message

use_case_id.description: The poll-mode use case that produced this message

event_id.description: Unique id of the originating event

enqueued_at.description: When the message was originally enqueued

delivery_attempts.description: Delivery attempts at the moment the message was dead-lettered

reason.description: Why the message was dead-lettered (policy or operator reason)

expires_at.description: When the DLQ entry expires (retention window re-armed at dead-letter time)

next_token.description: Opaque pagination token — present when more entries exist

ids.description: Message ids (msg_…) of the DLQ entries to redrive

status.description:
Outcome — `redriven` re-enqueued at the tail; `not_found` for
unknown ids or entries concurrently redriven/expired

results.description: Per-id redrive outcome, input order preserved

reason.description: Operator reason recorded on the dead-lettered head

unblocked.description: Whether a blocked head was skipped (false = no-op, stream was not blocked)

dead_lettered_id.description: Message id of the skipped head — present only when unblocked is true

operation.description:
Relation operation:
- '_set': Replace all existing relations with the specified items
- '_append': Add new unique items to existing relations (deduplicates by entity_id)
- '_append_all': Add all items to existing relations (no deduplication, allows duplicates)

jsonataExpression.description: JSONata expression that returns relation items array (alternative to 'items')

unique_ids.description: Unique identifier mappings for the related entity

attribute.description: Target attribute name in the related entity

field.description: Source field name from the event data

jsonataExpression.description: JSONata expression to compute the value

RelationRefsConfig.description:
Configuration for relation references ($relation_ref).
Relation references link to a specific item within a repeatable attribute on a related entity.
Common use case: referencing a specific address within a contact's address list.

operation.description:
Relation reference operation:
- '_set': Replace all existing relation_refs with the specified items
- '_append': Add new unique items to existing relation_refs (deduplicates by entity_id + _id)
- '_append_all': Add all items to existing relation_refs (no deduplication, allows duplicates)

items.description: Array of relation reference item configurations

jsonataExpression.description: JSONata expression that returns relation_ref items array (alternative to 'items')

RelationRefItemConfig.description: Configuration for a single relation reference item

unique_ids.description: Unique identifier mappings for the related entity

path.description: Attribute path on the related entity (e.g., "address")

RelationRefValueConfig.description: Configuration for the value to set on the related entity's attribute

attribute.description: Target attribute name on the related entity

operation.description:
Operation for the attribute value:
- '_set': Replace the attribute value
- '_append': Add new unique items (deduplicates)
- '_append_all': Add all items (no deduplication)

field.description: Source field name from the event data

jsonataExpression.description: JSONata expression to compute the value

RepeatableFieldType.description:
Type hint for repeatable fields that require special search handling.
These fields are stored as arrays of objects (e.g., email: [{ email: "value" }]).

event_ids.description: List of event IDs to replay. Maximum 100 events per request.

event_name.description: Filter by event name (alias for object_type)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

cursor.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the next_cursor from the previous response to get the next page.

event_time.description: Timestamp from the last event in the previous page

event_id.description: Event ID from the last event in the previous page

error_category.description: Filter by error category (only applicable when status=error)

event_name.description: Filter by event name (alias for object_type)

event_id.description: Filter by event ID to find a specific event

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

completed_at.description: Timestamp from the last event in the previous page

event_id.description: Event ID from the last event in the previous page

token_id.description:
Filter by a specific access token ID (e.g., 'api_5ZugdRXasLfWBypHi93Fk').
Must be one of the access_token_ids linked to the integration.
If omitted, returns logs for all access tokens linked to the integration.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

timestamp.description: Timestamp from the last log entry in the previous page

request_id.description: Request ID from the last log entry in the previous page

inbound_group_by.description: Fields to group inbound statistics by

outbound_group_by.description: Fields to group outbound statistics by

from_date.description: Start date for the time series (inclusive)

to_date.description: End date for the time series (inclusive). Defaults to current time if not specified.

interval.description: The time bucket interval for aggregation

direction.description: Filter by event direction. Defaults to both.

timestamp.description: The start timestamp of the bucket

inbound.description: Inbound event counts for this bucket. Null when direction is outbound.

outbound.description: Outbound event counts for this bucket. Null when direction is inbound.

event_name.description: Filter by event name (event_catalog_event). If not specified, returns events for all linked event names in the integration's outbound use cases.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

created_at.description: Timestamp from the last event in the previous page

event_id.description: Event ID from the last event in the previous page

metadata.description: Additional metadata (webhook_id, organization details, correlation_id, etc.)

updated_at.description: When the event was last updated

total_events.description: Total number of inbound events in the period

total_correlations.description: Total number of unique correlation IDs

last_error_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent error

breakdown.description: Statistics breakdown by requested inbound_group_by fields

total_events.description: Total number of outbound events in the period

last_error_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent error

breakdown.description: Statistics breakdown by requested outbound_group_by fields

correlation_id.description: Correlation ID for tracing related events

event_type.description: Type of event (optional for V3 events)

use_case_id.description: Filter by use case ID (UUID). Empty string matches "General" events. Special value "__unknown__" matches events whose use_case_id does not belong to any currently configured use case for this integration (excludes "General" events).

use_case_type.description: Filter by use case type (replaces direction)

code.description: Filter by taxonomy code (e.g. OAUTH2_TOKEN_FAILURE, HTTP_502)

correlation_id.description: Filter by correlation ID for tracing

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

cursor.description: Cursor for pagination (from previous response's next_cursor)

event_id.description: Trigger/inbound event ID (groups related monitoring events)

correlation_id.description: Correlation ID for tracing. Empty when unavailable.

use_case_id.description: Use case ID. Empty for system-level events ("General").

use_case_type.description: Use case type. Empty for system-level events.

code.description: Taxonomy code (e.g. OAUTH2_TOKEN_FAILURE, HTTP_502). Empty for success.

message.description: Human-readable message. Empty when not applicable.

detail.description: Event-specific JSON data. Structure determined by `code`.

created_at.description: When the monitoring event was created

use_case_type.description: Filter stats by use case type

group_by.description: Field to group the breakdown by

source.description: Data source for the stats. "monitoring" (default) aggregates processed events from erp_monitoring_v2 — this counts every event produced throughout the processing tree (fan-out children, post-actions, relation resolutions, etc.). "incoming" counts only the initial inbound events actually received (distinct event_id from erp_incoming_events); only group_by=use_case_id is supported and status/level breakdown is not available for this source (success/error/warning/skipped counts are returned as 0).

total_events.description: Total number of events in the period

ack_timeout_count.description: Number of ACK_TIMEOUT events (acknowledgement timed out)

last_error_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent error

breakdown.description: Statistics breakdown by requested group_by field

to_date.description: End of the time range (defaults to now)

group_by.description: Field to group the bucket breakdown by

use_case_id.description: Present when grouping by use_case. Unknown historical IDs are collapsed into "__unknown__"

success_count.description: Number of successful events in the breakdown item

error_count.description: Number of error events in the breakdown item

warning_count.description: Number of warning events in the breakdown item

skipped_count.description: Number of skipped events in the breakdown item

total_count.description: Total events in the breakdown item

success_count.description: Number of successful events in the bucket

error_count.description: Number of error events in the bucket

warning_count.description: Number of warning events in the bucket

skipped_count.description: Number of skipped events in the bucket

breakdown.description: Statistics breakdown by the requested group_by field for this bucket

next_cursor.description: Cursor to fetch the next page. Null if no more results.

has_more.description: Indicates if more results are available

GetMonitoringTimeSeriesResponse.description: Time-series aggregated event counts retrieved successfully

interval.description: The time bucket interval used for aggregation

from_date.description: Start date of the time series

to_date.description: End date of the time series

buckets.description: List of time-series buckets with event counts

next_cursor.description: Cursor to fetch the next page. Null if no more results.

has_more.description: Indicates if more results are available

next_cursor.description: Cursor to fetch the next page. Null if no more results.

has_more.description: Indicates if more results are available

event_ids.description: List of event IDs for which replay was requested

status.description: Processing status for the event (skipped indicates duplicate deduplication_id, ignored indicates unconfigured event)

TriggerWebhookResponse.description: Result of triggering an ERP synchronization event

next_cursor.description: Cursor to fetch the next page. Null if no more results.

has_more.description: Indicates if more results are available

next_cursor.description: Cursor to fetch the next page. Null if no more results.

has_more.description: Indicates if more results are available

GetAssociatedMonitoringEventsResponse.description: Associated monitoring events for the given event_id

monitoring_events.description: All monitoring events sharing this event_id, ordered by created_at ASC

inbound_event.description: The original inbound event payload from erp_incoming_events, if available
